description:
## Purpose

Define the scenario YAML schema using zod. This is the contract every fixture file conforms to; the harness rejects malformed scenarios up front. Per D-68 §Scenario YAML schema.

## Reference Implementation

- [colinhacks/zod](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod) — schema definition, refinements, custom errors. Read `src/types/index.ts` for the type-safe parsing pattern.
- [yeomanjs/yeoman-assert](https://github.com/yeomanjs/yeoman-assert) for the assertion vocabulary shape (file/fileContent/noFile).

## Files to Create

```
src/dev/
├── schema.ts                # zod schema
└── schema.test.ts
```

## Type Definitions

```typescript
const Scenario = z.object({
  name: z.string().min(1),
  description: z.string().optional(),
  input: z.string(),                          // dir name under tests/fixtures/inputs/
  args: z.array(z.string()).optional(),       // anvil CLI args (mutually exclusive with `pty`)
  env: z.record(z.string()).optional(),
  pty: z.object({                             // interactive scenario (TIX-000064)
    command: z.array(z.string()),
    script: z.array(z.union([
      z.object({ expect: z.string(), send: z.string() }),
      z.object({ expect_exit: z.number() }),
    ])),
  }).optional(),
  expect: z.object({
    exit_code: z.number().optional(),
    files_exist: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    files_absent: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    files_contain: z.array(z.object({ file: z.string(), matches: z.string() })).optional(),
    files_match_regex: z.array(z.object({ file: z.string(), pattern: z.string() })).optional(),
    stdout_contains: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    stderr_contains: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    stdout_empty: z.boolean().optional(),
    stderr_empty: z.boolean().optional(),
    files_unchanged_from_input: z.boolean().optional(),
  }),
}).refine(s => Boolean(s.args) !== Boolean(s.pty), {
  message: "scenario must specify exactly one of `args` or `pty`",
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Valid scenarios parse cleanly and produce typed objects.
- Invalid scenarios produce useful error messages naming the offending field.
- The `args` XOR `pty` constraint is enforced.
- Unit tests cover at least one valid scenario per input from TIX-000058.

completion.result: Implemented strict zod scenario schema with args/pty XOR, fixture input name validation, parser exports, and Bun contract tests.

description:
## Purpose

Implement the test harness core: load scenario YAML, copy `inputs/<name>/` to a temp directory, run anvil, capture stdout/stderr/exit-code, evaluate assertions, return a structured result. Per D-68.

## Reference Implementation

- [yeomanjs/yeoman-test](https://github.com/yeomanjs/yeoman-test) — `helpers.run()` pattern: temp dir per test, isolated execution, assertion-friendly result object. Read `src/index.ts`.
- [vercel/next.js `test/integration/create-next-app/`](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/tree/canary/test/integration/create-next-app) — patterns for spawning the CLI and asserting on the resulting tree.

## Files to Create

```
src/dev/
├── harness.ts               # core: copy input → run → assert → result
├── harness.test.ts
└── assertions.ts            # one function per `expect.*` key
```

## Type Definitions

```typescript
interface RunResult {
  scenario: string;
  passed: boolean;
  failures: string[];        // human-readable assertion failures
  exit_code: number;
  stdout: string;
  stderr: string;
  duration_ms: number;
  workdir: string;           // path to temp dir (for debugging)
}

async function runScenario(yamlPath: string): Promise<RunResult>;
```

## Required Behavior

- Resolve `input:` to `tests/fixtures/inputs/<name>/`; copy recursively to a fresh temp dir under `os.tmpdir()/anvil-fixtures-<random>/`.
- Spawn `bin/anvil` with `args` and `env` in the temp dir; capture all three streams.
- For each assertion in `expect:`, evaluate against the temp dir final state. Collect failures; do NOT short-circuit (run all assertions for full diagnostic output).
- `files_unchanged_from_input` uses the vendored `dir-compare` (TIX-000067) to compare temp dir against source input; pass only if no differences.
- On harness internal error (e.g., temp dir creation failed), throw — do not return `passed: false`. Distinguish bug from test failure.
- Cleanup: temp dir kept on failure (workdir in result for inspection); deleted on pass.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each input from TIX-000058 has a paired scenario in `tests/fixtures/scenarios/` that passes when run through the harness.
- Failure path is exercised: a deliberately-broken scenario (e.g., `exit_code: 99` against a successful run) reports a clear failure.
- Temp dirs survive on failure, are cleaned on success.

completion.result: Implemented scenario harness core with YAML loading, fixture copy, Bun CLI execution, assertion evaluation, cleanup semantics, and initial smoke scenarios for every fixture input.

description:
## Purpose

Implement `bun dev <scenario>` — the agent's primary inner-loop driver. Copies an input catalog dir into `.sandbox/scratch/` and prints the absolute path. The agent then `cd`s in and runs anvil manually. Per D-68 §Mode 1.

## Reference Implementation

- [tj/commander.js](https://github.com/tj/commander.js) — command/option/argument shape.
- Yeoman's `yo --help` UX for command discoverability.

## Files to Create

```
src/dev/
├── cli.ts                   # `bun dev` entry point (registered in package.json scripts)
└── cli.test.ts
.sandbox/.gitkeep
.gitignore                   # add `.sandbox/*\n!.sandbox/.gitkeep`
```

## Required Behavior

- `bun dev <scenario-name>` resolves `<scenario-name>` to a scenario YAML; copies its `input:` dir to `.sandbox/scratch/`.
- Default behavior: WIPE `.sandbox/scratch/` first.
- `--keep` flag: preserve existing `.sandbox/scratch/` contents (overlay copy).
- `--name <dirname>` flag: copy to `.sandbox/<dirname>/` instead of scratch (for parallel experiments).
- Print the absolute target path on stdout (so the agent can `cd $(bun dev greenfield)`).
- Print a one-line hint to stderr: "→ cd <path> && ../../bin/anvil ..."

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bun dev greenfield` creates `.sandbox/scratch/` matching `tests/fixtures/inputs/greenfield/`.
- `bun dev greenfield` twice in a row produces the same state (wipe-and-recopy).
- `bun dev greenfield --keep` preserves files added between runs.
- `.sandbox/scratch/` is gitignored.

completion.result: Implemented bun dev sandbox driver with scenario resolution, sandbox copy modes, package script, gitignore tracking, and cleanup-safe tests.

description:
## Purpose

Implement `bun fixtures` — runs all scenarios via the harness, reports pass/fail summary, exits non-zero on any failure. The mechanical regression gate. Per D-68 §Mode 2.

## Reference Implementation

- [yeomanjs/yeoman-test](https://github.com/yeomanjs/yeoman-test) integration with mocha — pattern for batch scenario execution.
- [vitest test reporter](https://github.com/vitest-dev/vitest) for inspiration on the summary output (don't pull it in — write minimally).

## Files to Modify

```
src/dev/cli.ts               # add `fixtures` subcommand
package.json                 # add `"fixtures": "bun src/dev/cli.ts fixtures"` script
```

## Required Behavior

- `bun fixtures` discovers all `tests/fixtures/scenarios/*.yaml`, runs each via the harness, prints per-scenario pass/fail line, and a final summary `(N passed, M failed in Xs)`.
- `--filter <substring>` runs only scenarios whose `name` matches the substring.
- On failure, print the failed scenario's `failures[]` block AND the path to its preserved temp dir for manual inspection.
- Exit code 0 on all pass; 1 on any fail.
- Output uses chalk for color but degrades gracefully when not a TTY.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bun fixtures` runs all scenarios from TIX-000058's catalog.
- A deliberately-broken scenario causes non-zero exit and clear failure output.
- `--filter drift` runs only drift-related scenarios.

completion.result: Implemented bun fixtures runner with scenario discovery, filter selection, failure reporting, package script wiring, focused tests, full-suite validation, typecheck, and sandbox cleanliness verification.

description:
## Purpose

Implement `bun agent:check` — runs only the fixtures touching files the agent has changed. The agent's tight, post-edit feedback signal. Silent on green. Per D-68 §CLI surface.

## Reference Implementation

- [vitest --changed](https://vitest.dev/guide/cli.html#changed) for the diff-based test selection pattern.
- [jest --findRelatedTests](https://jestjs.io/docs/cli#--findrelatedtests-spaceseparatedlistofsourcefiles) for scope-resolution heuristics.

## Files to Modify

```
src/dev/cli.ts               # add `agent:check` subcommand
src/dev/changed.ts           # logic for resolving changed files → relevant scenarios
src/dev/changed.test.ts
package.json                 # add `"agent:check": "bun src/dev/cli.ts agent:check"` script
```

## Required Behavior

- Run `git diff --name-only HEAD` to get changed files.
- For each scenario, mark it "relevant" if EITHER:
  - any file under its `input:` dir appears in changed files, OR
  - any file under `templates/` appears in changed files (templates feed all scenarios), OR
  - any file under `src/` appears in changed files (source changes affect all scenarios — fall back to full run).
- Run only relevant scenarios via the harness.
- Print nothing on full pass except a one-line `✓ N scenarios passed`.
- On failure: same output as `bun fixtures` (failures + workdir path).
- Non-zero exit on failure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Touching only `templates/typescript/Makefile.ejs` triggers TS scenarios but skips Go/Python scenarios.
- Touching `src/scaffold/render.ts` triggers all scenarios.
- Touching only `docs/foo.md` (untracked subsystem) skips ALL scenarios and exits 0 with `✓ 0 scenarios run`.

## Purpose

## Required Behavior

## Required Behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- The CI workflow file is valid YAML and triggers on PR + main push events.

description:
## Purpose

Vendor `proper-lockfile` to `src/internal/lockfile/`. Trim, port to TypeScript, preserve LICENSE, document provenance. Per D-67 §Part C.

## Reference Implementation

- [moxystudio/node-proper-lockfile](https://github.com/moxystudio/node-proper-lockfile) — read `lib/lockfile.js` end to end. The retry/stale-detection logic is the value; the rest is dependency adapter code we can drop.

## Files to Create

```
src/internal/lockfile/
├── LICENSE                  # copy from upstream (MIT)
├── README.md                # source repo, version pinned, what was kept/dropped, divergences
├── index.ts                 # public API (lock, unlock, check)
├── lockfile.ts              # core implementation
└── lockfile.test.ts
```

## Required Behavior

- Public surface: `lock(file, options?) → Promise<release>`, `unlock(file) → Promise<void>`, `check(file) → Promise<boolean>`.
- Core behaviors preserved from upstream:
  - Atomic lock via mkdir (or rename of a tmp file).
  - Stale lock detection via mtime + configurable threshold.
  - Retry policy with exponential backoff (use upstream's defaults).
- DROP from upstream:
  - graceful-fs adapter (use Node fs/promises directly).
  - Anything related to symlink workarounds for Windows that is no longer needed under modern Node.
  - cli wrapper (we don't need it).
- README documents: source URL, upstream version, what we kept, what we dropped, why.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tests cover: clean lock, double-lock fails, stale lock reclaimed, unlock idempotent.
- README clearly states provenance and divergence.
- LICENSE preserved verbatim.


## Path-Naming Convention (clarification)

`lock(file)` does NOT take a lock *on* `file`. It atomically creates a sibling directory `<file>.lock/` (the `.lock` suffix is appended). The protected `file` is left untouched by the lock primitive — callers are responsible for reading/writing it. This is the upstream proper-lockfile convention and must be preserved verbatim, since callers (TIX-000053) reason about the two paths as separate filesystem entries.

The vendored README MUST document this explicitly to prevent future maintainers from "fixing" it into looking like the lock and the file share a path.

completion.result: Vendored proper-lockfile v4.1.2 to src/internal/lockfile/ (327 LOC + 12 tests). Per D-67 §Part C. Reclaim race documented.

description:
## Purpose

Set up anvil's own self-governance per D-65: ship under an MIT LICENSE, automate releases via release-please on tag-triggered conventional-commit pipelines, and enforce conventional-commit format locally via commitlint. This is the "anvil dogfoods D-65" ticket — without it, anvil ships unlicensed and has no release pipeline.

This ticket complements TIX-000069 (which covers AGENTS.md self-dogfooding) and TIX-000027 (distribution — which lists `LICENSE` in `package.json#files` but did not previously create the file).

## Spec References

- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-65 (anvil dogfoods AGENTS.md, conventional commits, release-please, MIT LICENSE)
- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-69 (`googleapis/release-please` is the canonical reference for the release pipeline)
- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-66 (LICENSE for *scaffolded projects* deferred to v2 — but anvil's OWN LICENSE is in scope here)
- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-39 (no `anvil update`; release pipeline is the only update path for now)

## Background

D-65 makes three commitments about how anvil itself is developed and shipped:

1. **MIT LICENSE** at the repo root. Without this, anvil is technically un-distributable.
2. **Conventional commits** — every commit on `main` follows the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) spec (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, etc.). This drives release-please's automated changelog and version-bump logic.
3. **release-please** — every push to `main` runs the [google-github-actions/release-please-action](https://github.com/google-github-actions/release-please-action). It maintains an open release PR; merging the release PR creates a tag, generates a CHANGELOG entry, and publishes the tagged release.

Currently none of this exists. anvil has no LICENSE, no commitlint config, no release pipeline. This deliverable lands all three in one PR.

## Files to Create / Modify

```
LICENSE                                     # MIT (CREATE)
.github/workflows/release.yml               # release-please pipeline (CREATE)
release-please-config.json                  # release-please config (CREATE)
.release-please-manifest.json               # release-please manifest (CREATE)
commitlint.config.mjs                       # conventional-commits rules (CREATE)
.husky/commit-msg                           # commitlint hook entry point (CREATE)
package.json                                # add @commitlint/{cli,config-conventional}, husky as devDeps; add "prepare": "husky" script (MODIFY)
README.md                                   # add §Contributing with conventional-commits + release process (MODIFY)
```

### LICENSE

Standard MIT LICENSE text. Copyright holder: the anvil author/org (sourced from `package.json` `author` field — verify it's set in TIX-000017; otherwise this ticket sets it).

### .github/workflows/release.yml

```yaml
name: release-please

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        with:
          config-file: release-please-config.json
          manifest-file: .release-please-manifest.json
```

No additional secrets required — `GITHUB_TOKEN` is sufficient for tag/release creation in the same repo.

### release-please-config.json

```json
{
  "release-type": "node",
  "packages": {
    ".": {
      "release-type": "node",
      "include-component-in-tag": false,
      "draft": false,
      "prerelease": false
    }
  },
  "changelog-sections": [
    { "type": "feat", "section": "Features" },
    { "type": "fix", "section": "Bug Fixes" },
    { "type": "perf", "section": "Performance" },
    { "type": "refactor", "section": "Refactoring" },
    { "type": "docs", "section": "Documentation" },
    { "type": "chore", "section": "Chores", "hidden": true }
  ]
}
```

### .release-please-manifest.json

```json
{ ".": "0.1.0" }
```

(Initial version. release-please will bump this automatically.)

### commitlint.config.mjs

```js
export default {
  extends: ['@commitlint/config-conventional'],
};
```

### .husky/commit-msg

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
. "$(dirname -- "$0")/_/husky.sh"
bunx --bun commitlint --edit "$1"
```

### package.json modifications

- Add to `devDependencies`: `@commitlint/cli`, `@commitlint/config-conventional`, `husky`
- Add to `scripts`: `"prepare": "husky"`

### README.md §Contributing

Append a new top-level section:

```markdown
## Contributing

### Commit format

We follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/). Every commit on `main` must use:

    <type>(<optional scope>): <description>

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `chore`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `build`, `ci`.

The `commit-msg` hook (installed automatically via `bun install` → `husky`) validates this locally.

### Release process

Releases are automated via [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please-action). Every push to `main` updates an open release PR. To cut a release:

1. Merge the open `chore: release X.Y.Z` PR maintained by release-please.
2. release-please tags the merge commit and publishes the GitHub release with an auto-generated CHANGELOG.
3. No manual CHANGELOG edits — let release-please derive it from commit messages.

`git push --no-verify` skips the commit-msg hook locally; CI does not re-validate, so use sparingly and only for release-please's own commits.
```

## Tests

### Validation

Tests for this ticket are mostly file-presence + structural assertions (release-please workflow correctness is validated by the first real release):

1. **LICENSE exists and is MIT**: Read `LICENSE`; assert it starts with `MIT License` (or contains `Permission is hereby granted, free of charge`).
2. **release-please config is valid JSON**: Parse `release-please-config.json` and `.release-please-manifest.json`; assert no syntax errors.
3. **release-please workflow YAML is valid**: Parse `.github/workflows/release.yml` with the `yaml` dep; assert it has the `googleapis/release-please-action` step.
4. **commitlint config loads**: Import `commitlint.config.mjs` and assert it exports an `extends` array containing `@commitlint/config-conventional`.
5. **husky hook is executable**: Assert `.husky/commit-msg` exists and contains the `commitlint --edit "$1"` invocation.
6. **package.json wiring**: Assert `prepare` script is `husky`; assert `@commitlint/cli`, `@commitlint/config-conventional`, `husky` are all in `devDependencies`.
7. **Conventional-commit hook works locally** (integration; skip in CI if husky isn't installed): write a temp commit message file, invoke `bunx commitlint --edit <file>` with a known-bad message (`add feature`), assert exit 1; with a known-good message (`feat: add feature`), assert exit 0.
8. **README §Contributing exists**: Read README.md; assert it contains the substring `## Contributing` and references both `Conventional Commits` and `release-please`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `LICENSE` exists at the repo root containing MIT license text with the correct copyright holder
- [ ] `.github/workflows/release.yml` invokes `googleapis/release-please-action@v4` on push to `main`
- [ ] `release-please-config.json` and `.release-please-manifest.json` exist with valid JSON and the `release-type: node` config above
- [ ] `commitlint.config.mjs` extends `@commitlint/config-conventional`
- [ ] `.husky/commit-msg` is created and runs commitlint via `bunx`
- [ ] `package.json` adds `@commitlint/cli`, `@commitlint/config-conventional`, `husky` to devDependencies and a `prepare: husky` script
- [ ] `README.md` includes a `## Contributing` section documenting both the conventional-commits format and the release-please cutting process
- [ ] All 8 validation tests pass
- [ ] Running `bun install` installs husky and registers the commit-msg hook (smoke-tested via a known-bad commit message that the hook rejects)

## Reference Implementation

Per D-69:

- **`googleapis/release-please-action`** — canonical reference for the workflow + config layout. Study the README's "Configuration" section.
- **`conventional-changelog/commitlint`** — canonical reference for commitlint config and hook integration.
- **`typicode/husky`** — modern git-hook installer for Node/Bun projects. Husky v9+ uses the `husky` command (no `husky install`).

### Notes

- This ticket is bound by D-65 (Accepted, High-confidence). D-66 explicitly defers LICENSE generation for *scaffolded projects* to v2 — but anvil's OWN LICENSE is in scope here (D-65, not D-66).
- TIX-000027 (distribution) lists `LICENSE` in `package.json#files`; this ticket creates that file. Add this ticket to TIX-000027's `blockedBy` if release pipeline must precede first publish (recommended).
- `anvil update` is deferred to v2 (D-39); for now the only update path for users is to install a newer release via `bun install -g anvil@latest`. The release pipeline this ticket sets up is what makes those releases possible.

completion.result: MIT LICENSE, release-please v4 pipeline, commitlint+husky local guardrails, governance tests (8/8 pass). Per D-65.

description:
## Purpose

Ship a bundled toolchain-defaults snapshot at `src/internal/toolchain-defaults.json` so that `anvil init` can fall back to a deterministic last-known-good version when network resolution fails (D-64). This is the small piece that makes D-64 actually work without a network — no flag, no offline mode, just a graceful degradation.

## Spec References

- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-64 §Offline / network-failure mode
- `specs/decisions/anvil-decisions.md` — D-70 (related: lockfile pinning means re-scaffold is offline-safe by construction once the first init succeeds)

## Files to Create

```
src/internal/toolchain-defaults.json     # the snapshot
src/internal/toolchain-defaults.ts       # typed loader + types
src/internal/toolchain-defaults.test.ts  # validates schema + freshness check
scripts/refresh-toolchain-defaults.ts    # dev script: hits live endpoints, rewrites the JSON
```

## Required Behavior

- `toolchain-defaults.json` shape:

  ```json
  {
    "snapshotTakenAt": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z",
    "snapshotAnvilVersion": "0.1.0",
    "node": "22.11.0",
    "go": "1.23.4",
    "python": "3.13.0",
    "bun": "1.1.34"
  }
  ```

- `loadToolchainDefaults(): ToolchainDefaults` reads the bundled JSON via Bun's `import` (compile-time embedded).
- `scripts/refresh-toolchain-defaults.ts` hits the live endpoints (same ones D-64 lists) and rewrites the JSON. Run manually before each release; release-please workflow (TIX-000070) can optionally invoke it.
- Test: schema validation (each version is a non-empty semver-shaped string).
- Test: freshness check that fails if `snapshotTakenAt` is older than 90 days from `package.json` build date — forces a refresh on stale releases. (Implementation: read `snapshotTakenAt`, compute delta against the test's "now"; in CI, "now" is the commit date so this only fires when an old snapshot is being shipped to a new release.)

## Integration with Toolchain Resolution

When TIX-000023 (init) hits a network failure for any of `node`/`go`/`python`, it calls `loadToolchainDefaults()` and uses the bundled value, printing the documented warning per D-64 (`could not reach <source>; using bundled default <version> from anvil <anvil-version>`).

`bun` is resolved from `bun --version` first; the bundled `bun` value is only used when (a) Bun isn't locally installed AND (b) the GitHub release endpoint also fails.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/internal/toolchain-defaults.json` exists with all four version fields plus `snapshotTakenAt` and `snapshotAnvilVersion`
- [ ] `loadToolchainDefaults()` returns a typed `ToolchainDefaults` object
- [ ] Schema validation test passes for the shipped JSON
- [ ] Freshness test passes for a fresh snapshot, fails for a 91-day-old snapshot
- [ ] `scripts/refresh-toolchain-defaults.ts` regenerates the JSON when run; rerunning produces a stable result if the upstream sources haven't changed
- [ ] No production-dependency changes (uses only Node's `https` or Bun's `fetch` — no new npm deps)

completion.result: Bundled toolchain defaults snapshot, typed loader, freshness/schema tests, and refresh script added; validators passed.

Traceability: { sharedKey: "contributor-development-environments", specPath: "specs/toolchain/contributor-development-environments.md", requirementRefs: ["DEV-05", "DEV-06"], decisionRefs: ["D-71", "D-72"] }
Spec context: specs/toolchain/contributor-development-environments.md:20-25, 87-100, 125-129.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN a generated TypeScript project WHEN its Nix environment is entered and make check runs THEN only TypeScript-relevant tools plus shared security tools are present and make check fails if any are missing.
- GIVEN a generated Go project WHEN its Nix environment is entered and make check runs THEN only Go-relevant tools plus shared security tools are present and make check fails if any are missing.
- GIVEN a generated Python project WHEN its Nix environment is entered and make check runs THEN only Python-relevant tools plus shared security tools are present and make check fails if any are missing.
Must-haves: language-specific template files; manifest entries; e2e assertions for generated Nix files; README/AGENTS guidance in generated projects.
Adversarial tests: generated TS project does not include Go/Python-only tools; generated Go project does not include Python-only tools; generated Python project does not include Go-only tools.

description: Slice 3: Replace e2e/parity required-tool skips with hard environment failures
context:
Traceability: { sharedKey: "e2e-sandbox-environments", specPath: "specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md", requirementRefs: ["E2E-03", "E2E-04"], decisionRefs: ["D-71", "D-72", "D-73"] }
Spec context: specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md:20-32, 38-42, 123-129.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN a required tool is absent from a language e2e environment WHEN tests start THEN the run fails with an environment error rather than test.skip.
- GIVEN the Nix e2e environment is available WHEN tests/e2e and tests/parity run THEN TypeScript, Go, and Python checks execute with no required-tool skips.
- GIVEN normal contributor validation runs WHEN a supported-language check cannot run THEN the failure directs the contributor to the Nix entrypoint.
Must-haves: remove required-tool skip gates; central tool assertion helper; updated tests for failure messaging.
Adversarial tests: uv missing, gitleaks missing, govulncheck missing.

completion.result: Replaced supported-language e2e/parity required-tool skips with central hard-fail environment assertions, added Nix-entrypoint guidance, and validated TypeScript, Go, and Python e2e/parity checks in the Nix release shell with no required-tool skips.

description: Slice 4: Execute fixture setup.sh before Anvil runs
context:
Traceability: { sharedKey: "e2e-sandbox-environments", specPath: "specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md", requirementRefs: ["E2E-01"], decisionRefs: ["D-73"] }
Spec context: specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md:20-23, 54-62, 95-100, 123-126.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN a fixture input contains setup.sh WHEN bun fixtures runs THEN setup.sh executes after copy and before Anvil invocation.
- GIVEN setup.sh exits non-zero WHEN the scenario runs THEN the scenario fails and preserves a useful error.
- GIVEN dirty-git-repo and hostile fixtures WHEN their scenarios run THEN their setup effects are visible before Anvil starts.

completion.result: Executed fixture setup.sh from the copied sandbox before Anvil, surfaced setup failures with stdout/stderr while preserving workdirs, and updated dirty/hostile fixtures to assert setup-generated state.

description: Slice 5: Convert committed fixtures into real scaffold and re-scaffold scenarios
context:
Traceability: { sharedKey: "e2e-sandbox-environments", specPath: "specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md", requirementRefs: ["E2E-02", "E2E-06", "E2E-07"], decisionRefs: ["D-68", "D-73"] }

description: Slice 6: Make full-suite e2e execution deterministic and isolated
context:
Traceability: { sharedKey: "e2e-sandbox-environments", specPath: "specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md", requirementRefs: ["E2E-05"], decisionRefs: ["D-71", "D-72", "D-73"] }
Spec context: specs/toolchain/e2e-sandbox-environments.md:24-27, 108-129.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN repeated full bun test runs WHEN e2e executes alongside unit tests THEN temp dirs, HOME, caches, golangci-lint state, commit hook state, and PTY state do not race.
- GIVEN Go e2e make lint runs WHEN another test also uses golangci-lint THEN no parallel golangci-lint lock failure occurs.
- GIVEN fixture PTY scenarios run in full suite WHEN prompts complete THEN no timeout occurs because of shared global state.
Must-haves: per-test env isolation; cache/home isolation; locking or serialization where tools require it; repeated-run validation command.
Adversarial tests: concurrent Go lint invocations; commitlint hook test concurrent with temp git dirs; PTY scenario under full suite.

description: Slice 7: Add release CI no-skip validation gate
context:
Traceability: { sharedKey: "release-validation-and-distribution", specPath: "specs/toolchain/release-validation-and-distribution.md", requirementRefs: ["REL-01", "REL-02"], decisionRefs: ["D-71", "D-72", "D-74"] }
Spec context: specs/toolchain/release-validation-and-distribution.md:20-22, 48-62, 75-81, 119-126.
Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN release CI runs WHEN any required tool is unavailable THEN the job fails before reporting supported-language success.
- GIVEN release CI runs from a clean checkout THEN it runs bun agent:check, bun fixtures, bunx tsc --noEmit, full bun test, bun run build, supported-language e2e/parity, and bun mutation.
- GIVEN a supported-language e2e would skip THEN release CI fails.
Must-haves: workflow_dispatch and release/tag trigger; Nix release shell usage; no-skip assertions; artifacts/log retention.
Adversarial tests: intentionally remove uv/gitleaks/govulncheck in a controlled validation test; dirty worktree guard; flaky full-suite retry does not mask failure.

description: Slice 8: Prove standalone binary scaffolds outside the repository
context:

Acceptance Criteria:
- GIVEN a compiled host binary copied outside the repo WHEN anvil init --lang typescript --non-interactive runs THEN scaffold assets resolve and project files are generated.
